rounds.decision_reason: Applied bounded changes that directly target the observed map-path detour, heavy parser detour, and read/edit churn from the previous run.
Applied bounded parser-only changes that target the remaining schema and sqlite3-cli failure cluster while preserving the staged prompt-invariant skillpack.

Applied bounded prompt-invariant edits that front-load one-pass map resolution, remove the missing-binary `file` detour, and reduce post-snapshot read churn by steering solver work to civ6lib public APIs.
Applied prompt-invariant local edits that enforce hard district legality before scoring, reuse cached parsed-map artifacts after corrections, and stop immediately after the first verified output.
Applied bounded edits that directly target the observed map-path detour, unguarded SQLite probing, and repeated non-advancing retry cycles.
Applied bounded edits that directly target schema-mismatch churn, import-surface guessing, and non-required post-verification work.
Continue with the tightened skillpack because it now addresses the verifier-visible geothermal-fissure invalidation and the earlier Civ6 map parse churn without changing the invariant task prompt.

Continue with the prompt-invariant candidate because the staged skillpack now forces deterministic Civ6 map-path resolution and tighter open-failure recovery, which directly targets the round-3 setup churn.
Applied bounded skillpack changes that target the observed map-path and sqlite startup detour, the civ6lib import and result-shape mismatches, and the repeated custom-script patch loop.

Continue with the prompt-invariant candidate because the staged skillpack now adds a hard success-exit gate, foreground-first execution guidance, and explicit capability checks for process-inspection commands.

Continue with the prompt-invariant candidate because the staged skillpack now hard-gates canonical map resolution, parser-only `.Civ6Map` handling, and immediate termination once a directly verified solution has been written.

Continue with the prompt-invariant candidate because the staged skillpack now hard-gates exact civ6 API smoke tests before optimizer growth and treats post-write helper crashes or non-improving exhaustive search as non-reasons to reopen the loop.
Applied bounded changes that directly target the post-solution verification loop, missing sqlite preflight, and unchanged-tool churn from the previous run.
Applied bounded edits that cut parser retry churn, make the first solver timeout trigger a permanent fallback, and end on one verified finalization path.
Applied bounded parser-focused edits that make map-path normalization deterministic, gate sqlite backend choice once, and remove generic retrieval guidance that was fueling early churn.

Applied bounded handoff-focused edits that preflight overlay join keys, force a parser exit once base map artifacts exist, and keep later data requests targeted instead of reopening schema-debug loops.
The staged skillpack now targets the observed path-resolution, solver-churn, and final-regression failure modes with bounded local edits, so it should advance to the next round.
The staged skillpack now directly targets the observed bootstrap, import, and post-solution churn failures with three bounded local edits, so it should advance to the next round.
The staged skillpack now turns the observed timeout ladder, civ6lib contract mismatch, and background-polling churn into explicit bounded rules without changing the task prompt.
Applied contract-first parser and civ6lib guards plus a bounded one-solver plus one-verifier workflow to address the round-0 retry churn.

The staged skillpack now directly addresses the observed environment probe miss, the incremental validation bug, and the post-verification solver churn with three bounded local edits, so it should advance to the next round.

The staged skillpack now front-loads the Civ6 path/probe checklist, preserves the raw legality and adjacency contracts, and closes the run as soon as canonical JSON is written, so it should advance to the next round.
Applied bounded edits that directly target the observed import/API mismatch churn, unresolved SQLite path handling, and stale-edit rerun loop.

The staged skillpack now directly addresses the observed post-solution churn with a bounded close path, explicit output read-before-write handling, and a single authoritative Civ6 adjacency verification surface, so it should advance to the next round.
The staged skillpack now addresses the observed map-parser churn with a cached schema probe, sqlite row and column guardrails, and a bounded single-repair pass.

rounds.decision_reason: Applied bounded bootstrap, AC solver status, and AC-only reporting gates that directly target the observed pip detour, optimistic retry churn, and dc_optimal completion drift.
Applied bounded edits that front-load the baseline solver path, add interpreter-specific dependency preflight, and force an explicit pivot after non-advancing custom solver failures.
Applied bounded skillpack edits that tighten targeted discovery, merge the CasADi execution order with a harder pivot and infeasibility gate, and keep branch-flow guidance in an audit or debug role.

Applied a bounded rewrite of the CasADi skill so execution starts with interpreter and venv gating, forbids system pip fallback, and blocks final success until background solver work has fully completed and been verified.

The staged skillpack now addresses the observed environment bootstrap failure, unbounded solver rewrite churn, and missing AC-feasibility acceptance gate with bounded local edits, so it should advance to the next round.

The staged skillpack now front-loads environment and backend preflight, keeps recovery on a bounded canonical solver path, and closes the false-success feasibility loophole with local edits, so it should advance to the next round.

The staged skillpack now forces scoped network intake, adds a symbolic-integrity checkpoint before another solver rewrite, and explicitly guards against local name-shadowing of CasADi solver objects, so the next round should evaluate this tighter candidate.
The staged candidate now scopes discovery to task artifacts, bounds solver repair loops, and blocks fabricated feasibility or branch-loading reports after failed AC-OPF solves.
The staged candidate now makes the virtualenv preflight mandatory and blocks false-success reports unless totals, losses, and feasibility metrics agree after solve.

Applied a bounded rewrite that makes MATPOWER-style backend selection explicit, demotes custom CasADi to a fallback path, and adds tighter pivot and background-completion gates to cut solver thrash without changing the task prompt.
Continue with the prompt-invariant candidate that adds venv gating, bounded network inspection, and an early baseline pivot after repeated NaN/Jacobian failures.
Applied bounded skillpack edits that tighten exact-input discovery, enforce AC-only solver completion, and stop branch-flow helpers from validating DC or heuristic states as AC-feasible outcomes.
Continue with the prompt-invariant candidate that hard-gates exact-file discovery, venv-only dependency recovery, and non-advancing solver edits before the next AC baseline attempt.
Continue with the prompt-invariant candidate that adds venv gating, known-path data discovery, and one-run solver/completion guardrails for CasADi ACOPF execution.

Continue with the prompt-invariant candidate that front-loads exact-input and venv gates, adds a ready real-valued CasADi scaffold, and replaces brittle inline report inspection with a scripted helper.
Continue with the prompt-invariant candidate that preserves the canonical AC OPF case and blocks relaxed-case or dropped-constraint reruns after repeated solver failures.
The staged next skillpack now adds bounded discovery, venv-first execution, and an AC-only completion gate that directly addresses the round-0 false-success pattern.
The staged next skillpack now tightens large-file discovery, interpreter pinning, and edit/progress guardrails; it should be evaluated as the round-2 candidate.

The staged next skillpack now closes the remaining async-completion, bounded-output, and failure-triage gaps; it should be evaluated as the round-3 candidate even though round-2 already achieved reward 1.0.
